steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence structure around the blank: the text before the blank ends with "via the magazine ______" and must be followed by a description of the magazine ("Published from 1866 to 1899 and distributed throughout [cities], this magazine helped make Braddon's serialized novels globally available"). The blank must create a proper sentence boundary between two independent clauses while also introducing the magazine's name.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — *Belgravia*, published: A comma before "published" creates a comma splice — two independent clauses joined by only a comma. ✗
  • Choice B — *Belgravia*, which was published: This adds a relative clause, but it still joins the first independent clause to "this magazine helped..." with only a comma. ✗
  • Choice C — *Belgravia*. Published: The period properly ends the first independent clause after the magazine name. The next sentence then opens with the participial phrase "Published from 1866 to 1899..." modifying "this magazine," followed by the main clause. Grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice D — *Belgravia* published: No punctuation between the two clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗
C is correct.
